summary: Audit Log v2 architecture — replaces v1 single-table design (legacy)
description_md:
As a workspace-admin, I want the audit log system to use a new v2 architecture with hash-chained entries and automatic archival, so that compliance requirements are met and historical audit data is retained cost-effectively.

## Background

This story REPLACES the legacy v1 audit log architecture which used a single Postgres table without cryptographic signing or archival capabilities. The new v2 architecture implements hash-chained log entries for tamper detection and automatically archives logs to S3 Glacier after 90 days of retention.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Audit log entries are hash-chained such that each entry includes a cryptographic hash of the previous entry, preventing undetectable tampering
2. Audit log entries older than 90 days are automatically archived to S3 Glacier with retrieval working within the defined SLA
3. Each audit log entry includes a digital signature that can be verified by the compliance officer
4. Existing v1 audit log data is migrated to v2 format with hash-chain reconstruction, maintaining data integrity

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Hash-chaining implementation complete. Migration script tested with 500K legacy entries - all verified.
